title: Dynamic Head Selection for Efficient Attention

slides.assertion_title: Dynamic Head Selection Improves Attention Efficiency
slides.takeaway: Learned gating reduces FLOPs by 29% while improving accuracy.
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome to this presentation on our dynamic head selection method.

slides.assertion_title: Standard attention scales quadratically with sequence length
slides.takeaway: Real-world deployment is limited by computational cost.
slides.speaker_notes: The quadratic complexity of standard self-attention limits its application to long sequences.

slides.assertion_title: Existing sparse attention sacrifices accuracy for speed
slides.takeaway: A new approach is needed that preserves accuracy.
body_lines: Prior work: trade accuracy for speed
slides.speaker_notes: Prior approaches like sparse attention and Linformer reduce compute but sacrifice accuracy on hard examples.

slides.assertion_title: Dynamic head selection: learn which heads to use per input
slides.takeaway: First learned gating mechanism for attention head selection.
slides.speaker_notes: Our key contribution is a learned gating mechanism that dynamically selects attention heads per input.

slides.assertion_title: Gating module filters inactive heads before attention computation
slides.speaker_notes: The method flow: input embeddings are scored by a lightweight gating module, which selects active heads for sparse attention computation.

slides.assertion_title: Gating module uses lightweight relevance scoring
slides.speaker_notes: The gating module computes a relevance score for each attention head and applies a learned threshold to determine activation.
